word: H6914 - קִבְרֹות הַתַּאֲוָה - Qibrôwth hat-Taʼă-vâh - kib-roth' hat-tahav-aw' - Kibroth-hattaavah

sections.paragraph:
Original: קברות התּאוה

Transliteration: qibrôth hatta'ăvâh

Phonetic: kib-roth' hat-tah-av-aw'

BDB Definition:

Kibroth-hattaavah = "graves of lust"
  1. a station of Israel in the wilderness 3 campsites away from Sinai near the gulf of Akabah

Origin: from the fem. pl. of H6913 and H8378 with the article interposed

TWOT entry: None

Part(s) of speech:
